title: Doubled prefix operators !! and ~~ should be avoided
description: Doubled prefix operators "!!" and "~~" should not be usedAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearreliabilityBugQuick FixIDE quick fixes available with SonarQube for IDEWhy is this an issue?The repetition of a prefix operator (!, or~) is usually a typo. The second operator invalidates the first one.int v1 = 0; bool v2 = false; var v3 = !!v1; // Noncompliant: equivalent to "v1" var v4 = ~~v2; // Noncompliant: equivalent to "v2"Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CI©2025SonarSource Sàrl. All rights reserved.Privacy Policy|Cookie Policy|Terms of Use
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.reasoning: Doubled prefix operators (!! and ~~) are usually typos where the second operator cancels the first, making the code equivalent to no operator at all. This is confusing and likely a mistake.
